title: pittosporum tenuifolium var. fasciculatum
intro: Pittosporum tenuifolium var. fasciculatum is a plant that belongs to the Pittosporaceae family and is commonly known as the 'New Zealand tobacco tree' due to its leaves that resemble tobacco. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, primarily due to its leaves and bark, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The leaves and bark of the plant have been reported to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum tenuifolium var. fasciculatum can be made by infusing the leaves and bark in hot water to create a tea, or by drying and powdering them to be used in capsules or tinctures.
